user_goals_summary: This user writes dense, formally rigorous academic prose and cares most about argumentative architecture: every section must earn its place by naming the problem it responds to and cashing out what its machinery gains. Formalism is valued highly but treated as a means to argumentative ends, not an end in itself. The user expects formal register throughout, with rare, disciplined moments of plain-language directness that strengthen rather than undermine the reader's trust in the technical apparatus.

rubric.name: Inter-section Dependency Logic
rubric.description: Each section or subsection opens by naming the specific problem, constraint, or unresolved question that the preceding section established. Design choices are presented as forced consequences of prior constraints, not arbitrary selections, so the reader can reconstruct the argument's order from the dependency chain alone.

dimensions.label: Each subsection opens by identifying the specific problem or limitation the previous section created
Design choices are framed as necessary responses to established constraints, not as preferences among alternatives
A reader could reconstruct why sections appear in their order solely from the dependency logic
Components within a section that could read as a parallel list are instead presented as a logical sequence where each is necessitated by a property of the prior

rubric.name: Subsection Argumentative Arc
rubric.description: Each subsection follows a motivation–machinery–payoff arc: it opens by framing the problem the reader should care about before introducing any formal apparatus, and closes with a sentence or two that cashes out what the section's design gains for the larger argument. The reader always knows *why* before encountering *what*.

dimensions.label: Subsection opens with the motivating problem or design challenge before any definitions or formal machinery
Subsection closes with a sentence that states what the section's approach gains or enables for the broader project
The reader understands why they are reading each piece before encountering what it formally specifies
No subsection ends at the formalism — every section steps back to connect machinery to its argumentative purpose

rubric.name: Formalism in Service of Argument
rubric.description: Formal definitions, notation, and technical machinery are introduced in direct response to a stated problem and are explicitly connected to their argumentative purpose. The formalism resolves or sharpens a question the surrounding prose has raised; it is never self-standing or presented as its own justification.

dimensions.label: Each piece of formal notation or machinery is introduced in response to a named problem, not presented self-standingly
Formal results or definitions are connected back to what they mean for the argument or design
Empirical or behavioral claims that underpin the formalism are flagged for evidential grounding (e.g., citation placeholders)
Notation and formal apparatus sharpen or resolve a question rather than restating in symbols what prose already said

rubric.name: Strategic Plain-Language Moments
rubric.description: In dense formal passages, one or two sentences of plain, direct language restate what the formalism *means* — not what it specifies — strengthening the reader's trust that the author commands the machinery. These moments are brief (one sentence), arrive only after the formal specification they restate, and return immediately to formal register. The contrast is what makes them effective.

dimensions.label: Plain-language restatements appear after the formal specification they summarize, never before
Each plain-language aside is at most one or two sentences — brief enough to feel like a moment of clarity, not a mode shift
The plain restatement articulates what the formalism means or implies, not what it technically specifies
Formal register resumes immediately after the plain-language moment without lingering or expanding the aside
Plain-language moments are placed at points of maximum density or conceptual payoff, not distributed uniformly

rubric.name: Formal Register and Precision
rubric.description: Language is precise and academic throughout, with no casual phrasing, colloquialisms, or unnecessary hedging. Terminology is consistent across sections. Register shifts are controlled, deliberate, and visibly marked (e.g., 'put simply'), never accidental or gradual.

dimensions.label: No colloquialisms, casual transitions, or informal vocabulary appear outside of marked plain-language moments
Technical terms and names for concepts are used consistently across all sections
Any departure from formal register is explicitly signaled (e.g., 'put plainly,' 'put simply') and visibly intentional
Word choices are specific and exact, avoiding vague or generic academic filler (e.g., 'clear and effective')

coaching_notes: This user diagnoses structural issues with exceptional precision — they identify the exact sentence where an argument falters and articulate the principle it violates. They value logical dependency above all: every piece of writing must feel like a chain of forced consequences, not a sequence of independent choices. When giving feedback, they consistently frame issues as 'the implicit thing that needs to be made explicit,' so surfacing latent logical connections is the single highest-leverage move. They accept formalism readily and never ask for simplification, but they will push back hard if formalism is not in service of an argumentative claim.

user_goals_summary: The user writes formally structured methodology sections for technically sophisticated venues. They value writing that reads as a sustained argument rather than a technical manual — every subsection and paragraph must earn its position through explicit dependency logic connecting it to what precedes and follows. rubric.name: Subsection Argumentative Arc
rubric.description: Each subsection follows a motivation–machinery–payoff arc: it opens by framing the problem the reader should care about before introducing any formal apparatus, and closes by cashing out what the section's design gains for the larger argument — ideally naming what becomes possible downstream. The reader always knows *why* before encountering *what*.

dimensions.label: Subsection opens with the motivating problem or design challenge before any definitions or formal machinery
Subsection closes with a sentence that states what the section's approach gains, enables, or makes possible for downstream sections
The reader understands why they are reading each piece before encountering what it formally specifies
No subsection ends at the formalism — every section steps back to connect machinery to its argumentative purpose

rubric.name: Dependency Logic Across All Levels
rubric.description: Every transition — between subsections and between paragraphs within a subsection — names the specific problem, constraint, or unresolved question that the preceding unit established. Seams are load-bearing: design choices are framed as forced consequences of prior constraints, and a reader should feel that the text could not be reordered without breaking the causal chain.

dimensions.label: Each unit opens by identifying the specific problem, limitation, or open variable the previous unit created
Transitions include an explicit causal clause naming what prior content established that forces the current question (e.g., 'Because X defined above...')
Design choices are framed as necessary responses to established constraints, not as preferences among alternatives
A reader could reconstruct why units appear in their order solely from the dependency logic — no paragraph could be moved without breaking the chain
Components that could read as a parallel list are instead presented as a logical sequence where each is necessitated by a specific property of the prior

dimensions.label: Plain-language restatements appear only after the formal specification they summarize, never before
Each plain-language aside is at most one or two sentences — brief enough to feel like a moment of clarity, not a mode shift
The plain restatement articulates what the formalism means or implies, not what it technically specifies
Formal register resumes immediately after the plain-language moment without lingering or expanding the aside
Plain-language moments are rare within a section (one or two at most), placed at points of maximum density, and not repeated if already used nearby

coaching_notes: Non-negotiable: every transition must be load-bearing — the user will repeatedly flag any paragraph or subsection that could be reordered without breaking the argument. The user treats 'reads like a list' as the cardinal structural failure. Plain-language moments are valued but strictly rationed: one per section maximum, varied in marker phrasing, and only after formal machinery is established. The user's revision instinct is to push the same structural principle (dependency logic, argumentative arc) deeper into finer-grained units — from section level to subsection level to paragraph level — so drafts should anticipate this pressure at every level of granularity from the start.

Every structural unit must justify its existence through causal dependency on what precedes it, and every piece of formal machinery must be motivated before introduction and cashed out after specification. Dense formalism is punctuated by rare, disciplined moments of plain language that demonstrate command rather than concession.
coaching_notes: Non-negotiable: every structural unit must orient the reader to its purpose before any technical content begins — this applies at both the subsection and paragraph level. The user treats dependency logic as the primary quality signal; a passage that is technically correct but could be reordered without consequence reads as broken. Plain-language moments are valued precisely because they are rare and disciplined — the user will reject them if they overstay or arrive before the formalism they restate. Closings that summarize are treated as failures; the user expects closings that open the next argumentative door.

user_goals_summary: The user writes methodology sections for technically sophisticated venues (e.g., ACL) and values writing that reads as a sustained argument rather than a procedural manual. Every design choice must feel logically forced by constraints the prior section established, formalism must be framed by the problem it resolves and the capability it enables, and the formal register should be interrupted only by rare, precisely placed plain-language moments that demonstrate intuitive command of the machinery.

rubric.name: Subsection Argumentative Arc
rubric.description: Each subsection and each paragraph within it follows a motivation–machinery–payoff arc: it opens by framing the problem the reader should care about before introducing any formal apparatus, and closes by cashing out what the design gains for the larger argument. Closings create forward pressure — opening the next argumentative door — rather than summarizing what was just established.

dimensions.label: Each unit (subsection or paragraph) opens with the motivating problem, design challenge, or orienting question before any definitions or formal machinery
Each unit closes by naming what the approach enables or makes possible downstream — creating forward pressure rather than summarizing what was just decided
The reader understands why they are reading each piece before encountering what it formally specifies
No unit ends at the formalism — every section steps back to connect machinery to its argumentative purpose

rubric.name: Dependency Logic Across All Levels
rubric.description: Every transition — between subsections and between paragraphs within a subsection — names the specific problem, constraint, or unresolved question that the preceding unit established. Seams are load-bearing: design choices are framed as forced consequences of prior constraints, not preferences among alternatives. Transitions name enabling conditions rather than completed actions — 'X was necessary precisely so that Y becomes possible' rather than 'having done X, we do Y.' A reader should feel that the text could not be reordered without breaking the causal chain.

dimensions.label: Each unit (subsection or paragraph) opens by identifying the specific problem, limitation, or open variable the previous unit created
Transitions name the enabling condition that makes the current step possible or necessary, not merely the completed action that preceded it (causal framing, not temporal sequencing)
Design choices are framed as necessary responses to established constraints, not as preferences among alternatives
A reader could reconstruct why units appear in their order solely from the dependency logic — no paragraph or subsection could be moved without breaking the chain
Components that could read as a parallel list are instead presented as a logical sequence where each is necessitated by a specific property of the prior

rubric.name: Formalism in Service of Argument
rubric.description: Formal definitions, notation, and technical machinery are introduced in direct response to a stated problem and are explicitly connected to their argumentative purpose. The formalism resolves or sharpens a question the surrounding prose has raised; it is never self-standing or presented as its own justification.

dimensions.label: Each piece of formal notation or machinery is introduced in response to a named problem, not presented self-standingly
Formal results or definitions are connected back to what they mean for the argument or design

Notation and formal apparatus sharpen or resolve a question rather than restating in symbols what prose already said

rubric.name: Formal Register and Precision
rubric.description: Language is precise and academic throughout, with no casual phrasing, colloquialisms, or unnecessary hedging. Terminology is consistent across sections. Register shifts are controlled, deliberate, and visibly marked (e.g., 'put simply'), never accidental or gradual.

dimensions.label: No colloquialisms, casual transitions, or informal vocabulary appear outside of marked plain-language moments
Technical terms and names for concepts are used consistently across all sections
Any departure from formal register is explicitly signaled (e.g., 'put plainly,' 'put simply') and visibly intentional
Word choices are specific and exact, avoiding vague or generic academic filler

rubric.name: Strategic Plain-Language Moments
rubric.description: In dense formal passages, one or two sentences of plain, direct language restate what the formalism *means* — not what it specifies — strengthening the reader's trust that the author commands the machinery deeply enough to step back from it. These moments are brief, arrive only after the formal specification they restate, return immediately to formal register, and are rare enough within a subsection to feel like moments of clarity rather than a habit or a mode shift.
